title_en: Taharah in Mughni al-Muhtaj: Detailed Analysis
title_ar: الطهارة في مغني المحتاج: تحليل تفصيلي
content_en:
The taharah chapter in Mughni al-Muhtaj is among the most detailed treatments of purification law in the Shafi'i tradition, providing ash-Shirbini's careful explanation of an-Nawawi's condensed formulations in the Minhaj alongside the relevant hadith evidence and comparative scholarly opinions.

Ash-Shirbini begins by explaining the linguistic meaning of taharah — removal of impurity (najasah) or lifting of an impediment (hadath) — before moving to its legal definition. He then follows the Minhaj's classification of water into the categories familiar from Shafi'i jurisprudence, with particular attention to the two-qullah threshold. The measure of two qullahs is explained as approximately 500 Baghdad ritl in volume, which ash-Shirbini relates to a vessel roughly two and a half cubits in height, width, and depth. He notes that when the exact amount is uncertain, one takes the smaller estimate (the amount that favors the continuation of impurity) following the principle of caution in matters of worship.

On the impurity categories, ash-Shirbini examines the three-tier Shafi'i system (mughallazah, mukhaffafah, mutawassitah) in detail. The heavy impurity of dog and pig contact is discussed with full attention to the evidential basis: the sevenfold washing with earth for dog saliva derives from the hadith in Sahih Muslim, and ash-Shirbini examines why this ruling extends to the saliva, not just the breath, of the dog. He also explores the question of whether the pig's impurity is established by direct hadith or by analogical extension from the dog — noting this as an internal Shafi'i scholarly debate.

The treatment of wudu in Mughni al-Muhtaj is exceptionally thorough. Ash-Shirbini explains the Shafi'i requirement of intention (niyyah) as the first fard element of wudu, tracing it to the universal hadith principle 'actions are by intentions' (innama al-a'malu bin-niyyat — Bukhari, Muslim). The Shafi'i school's position is that the intention must accompany the first obligatory act of wudu (washing the face); it cannot be made at the beginning of the sunnahs before the first fard act. He explains the Hanafi position (that intention is sunnah, not fard, in wudu) and addresses the evidence adduced by each side.

The requirement of order (tartib) in wudu is another distinctively Shafi'i position explained in detail. Ash-Shirbini notes that while the Quran's verse (al-Ma'idah 5:6) presents the four acts of wudu in a specific sequence, the Arabic conjunction waw does not inherently require sequence. The Shafi'i requirement of order is derived instead from the Prophet's consistent practice (reported in numerous authenticated hadiths) of always performing wudu in the Quranic order, combined with the general principle that the Prophet's worship practices establish the obligatory method.

On ghusl, the Minhaj and its commentary distinguish carefully between the three fard elements (intention, reaching all skin and hair roots with water, and removing any barrier) and the extensive sunnahs (beginning with wudu, washing the right side first, using sufficient water without excess, reciting appropriate supplications). The commentary addresses edge cases that are technically important: does water need to reach under a ring or watch? What about tightly braided hair? Ash-Shirbini's responses follow the Shafi'i principle that anything preventing water from reaching the skin must be removed or loosened during ghusl.

Mughni al-Muhtaj's taharah chapter concludes with extensive treatment of the purification of garments and places, the threshold quantities for different types of impurity, and the principles governing doubt in purification matters — all grounded in the Shafi'i school's careful balancing of the hadith evidence with systematic legal principles.
title_id: Thaharah dalam Mughni al-Muhtaj: Analisis Terperinci
content_id:
Bab thaharah dalam Mughni al-Muhtaj adalah di antara perlakuan-perlakuan paling terperinci tentang hukum thaharah dalam tradisi Syafi'i, menyediakan penjelasan asy-Syirbini yang cermat atas formulasi ringkas an-Nawawi, memperluas argumen-argumennya, dan menangani kasus-kasus yang muncul dari pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang di mana batas-batas dari berbagai kondisi thaharah berada.

Perlakuan terhadap wudhu sangat terperinci: asy-Syirbini menjelaskan setiap unsur definisi an-Nawawi tentang wudhu yang sah — kondisi-kondisi niat, cara yang tepat untuk membasuh setiap bagian, dan kondisi-kondisi yang membuatnya tidak sah — dengan presisi yang menunjukkan pengetahuannya tentang seluruh tradisi hukum Syafi'i tentang topik ini. Diskusinya tentang kasus-kasus yang meragukan — di mana seseorang tidak yakin apakah thaharah telah batal, apakah suatu zat adalah najis, atau apakah air cukup untuk thaharah — sangat berharga karena kasus-kasus inilah yang para pelajar yang mencari bimbingan hukum paling sering hadapi.

Bagian-bagian tentang mandi wajib (ghusl) dan tayamum mengikuti pola yang sama, dengan asy-Syirbini memberikan penjelasan yang menyeluruh atas Al-Minhaj an-Nawawi dan menangani pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang muncul dari kondisi-kondisi yang tidak biasa. Perlakuan terhadap mandi wajib sangat berguna untuk memahami cara madzhab Syafi'i menangani kasus-kasus kompleks seperti mandi wajib dalam kondisi cuaca yang sangat dingin, mandi wajib ketika sebagian anggota badan tidak dapat dibasuh karena luka, dan kondisi-kondisi lain yang memerlukan panduan dari seorang ahli hukum yang terlatih.
content_ar:
يُعالج الشربيني في مغني المحتاج كتابَ الطهارة بعمق وتفصيل يعكسان مكانة الطهارة بوصفها مدخلًا لصحة العبادات في الفقه الشافعي. وتشمل الطهارة في الفقه الشافعي: الوضوء والغسل والتيمم وإزالة النجاسة.

ويُفصّل الشربيني في شروط الوضوء وفرائضه وسننه ومكروهاته ومبطلاته، مستندًا في كل مسألة إلى نص المنهاج ثم موضحًا مراد النووي وراجحَ المذهب. ومن المسائل الدقيقة التي يُعالجها: المسح على الخفين وشروطه، وحكم استعمال الماء المستعمل في الطهارة، والاجتهاد في القبلة عند الغيم.

وفي باب الغسل يذكر موجباته الواجبة والمستحبة وفرائضه وسننه، مع تقرير الموضع الراجح في المذهب عند التعارض بين الوجهين. وفي التيمم يُقيّد شروطه ومواضع مسحه وما يُبيح التيمم من عجز عن استعمال الماء.

ويمتاز تناوله للطهارة بالاتساق مع القواعد العامة للمذهب وبالإحكام في التعريفات مما يجعله مرجعًا أول للمفتي والمدرس.
